{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,

    "RX.3": 1.0,

    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.6",
    "R1.7",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "For this section review, should the focus be A) technical/content review (conceptual framing, logic, evidence alignment, novelty), B) writing/structure polish, or C) citation/related-work coverage? Please pick one primary focus (or rank them).",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Assess whether the section clearly frames the contribution and motivation of DRouter and its claimed co-designed routing approach.",
    "R1.2": "Check that the argument from heterogeneous workers and queue-aware admission to improved p99 latency is logically coherent and well-supported within the text.",
    "R1.3": "Verify that stated results (e.g., 38% lower p99, MAE 41 tokens) appropriately support the claims made and are not overextended beyond what is reported.",
    "R1.4": "Evaluate the claimed novelty (e.g., 'first' to co-design cost prediction with admission control) relative to nearest known work at a high level.",
    "R1.6": "Review sentence- and paragraph-level clarity and style, noting long or complex constructions that may hinder readability.",
    "R1.7": "Check whether references and attributions are sufficient to substantiate claims (especially the 'first' claim) and that section cross-references are accurate.",
    "RX.1": "Flag where conclusions depend on unspecified details (e.g., §4 evaluation not shown) and indicate uncertainty accordingly.",
    "RX.2": "Guard against endorsing overbroad claims, particularly attribution of gains solely to the admission policy and the 'first' assertion.",
    "RX.3": "Keep the assessment limited to the provided section without inferring beyond the given text or unseen experiments.",
    "RX.4": "Avoid feedback that could encourage misleading performance claims or operational practices that might harm downstream users or systems.",
    "RX.5": "Tie observations to specific sentences or claims in this section (e.g., '38% lower p99', 'first' claim, classifier MAE) for traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: For this section review, should the focus be A) technical/content review (conceptual framing, logic, evidence alignment, novelty), B) writing/structure polish, or C) citation/related-work coverage? Please pick one primary focus (or rank them).

rationale.R1.1: Assess whether the section clearly frames the contribution and motivation of DRouter and its claimed co-designed routing approach.
rationale.R1.2: Check that the argument from heterogeneous workers and queue-aware admission to improved p99 latency is logically coherent and well-supported within the text.
rationale.R1.3: Verify that stated results (e.g., 38% lower p99, MAE 41 tokens) appropriately support the claims made and are not overextended beyond what is reported.
rationale.R1.4: Evaluate the claimed novelty (e.g., 'first' to co-design cost prediction with admission control) relative to nearest known work at a high level.
rationale.R1.6: Review sentence- and paragraph-level clarity and style, noting long or complex constructions that may hinder readability.
rationale.R1.7: Check whether references and attributions are sufficient to substantiate claims (especially the 'first' claim) and that section cross-references are accurate.
rationale.RX.1: Flag where conclusions depend on unspecified details (e.g., §4 evaluation not shown) and indicate uncertainty accordingly.
rationale.RX.2: Guard against endorsing overbroad claims, particularly attribution of gains solely to the admission policy and the 'first' assertion.
rationale.RX.3: Keep the assessment limited to the provided section without inferring beyond the given text or unseen experiments.
rationale.RX.4: Avoid feedback that could encourage misleading performance claims or operational practices that might harm downstream users or systems.
rationale.RX.5: Tie observations to specific sentences or claims in this section (e.g., '38% lower p99', 'first' claim, classifier MAE) for traceability.
